description: Cross-target conformance test vectors for the Tonnetz engine (see docs/ai/adr/archive/001-tonnetz-engine-interface.md and docs/ai/adr/003-m4l-parameters-state.md). All target implementations (m4l, vst, app) must pass these. Binding assertion: pitch-class set of output equals expected_pcs (order-independent). Realized octaves may vary by integer multiples of 12 due to target-specific boundary behavior in buildTriad; pitch-class identity is the contract.

conventions.triad: [a, b, c] MIDI note numbers. For inputs in close position, a=root, b=root+3 or +4, c=root+7. identify_triad cases also include inversions.
conventions.pitch_classes: integer 0..11 (C=0, ..., B=11), sorted ascending for set equality
conventions.transforms: P | L | R. Sequences of single-char strings.
conventions.cells: Each cell is one of 'P' | 'L' | 'R' | 'hold'. Sequencer iterates cells cyclically; one cell consumed per stepsPerTransform host ticks. 'hold' leaves walker on the current triad.
conventions.jitter: float 0..1. Per-step probability that the cell's op is replaced by a uniform-random pick from {P, L, R, hold}.
conventions.seed: int. Seeds a mulberry32 PRNG. For each transform tick, two floats are sampled: first decides substitute-or-not (substitute iff r < jitter); second selects the substitute op (idx = floor(r * 4) ∈ {0=P, 1=L, 2=R, 3=hold}). The PRNG is seeded fresh from `seed` at the start of every walk(state, pos) call so any-pos restart reproduces.

apply_transform.name: P on first inversion C major
L on second inversion C major

walk_deterministic.name: PLR cycle, jitter=0, spt=1
walk_deterministic.description: Plain cell cycle with no holds and no jitter. cell index advances on every host tick.

walk_deterministic.name: hold cells preserve chord
walk_deterministic.description: 'hold' cells leave the walker untouched. Verifies the four-element op space.

walk_deterministic.name: stepsPerTransform=2 holds chord between cells
walk_deterministic.description: Cells consumed only at boundaries (every spt host ticks). Between boundaries, walker holds.

walk_deterministic.name: all-hold cells freeze startChord
walk_deterministic.description: Pathological case: every cell is hold. Walker never moves.

walk_deterministic.name: single-cell program loops one op
walk_deterministic.description: cells.length=1 case. The same op applies on every transform tick.

mulberry32.description: PRNG used for jitter. Standard mulberry32 (https://gist.github.com/tommyettinger/46a3c5e1ba2554c7b2f6dab3f54e1eb6). Each implementation MUST produce these exact floats from the given seed for the first N calls. This is the binding contract for cross-target jitter reproducibility.
mulberry32.algorithm: function mulberry32(seed) { let a = seed >>> 0; return () => { a = (a + 0x6D2B79F5) | 0; let t = a; t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1); t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61); return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296; } }
mulberry32.samples: FILLED_BY_ENGINE_IMPLEMENTATION (run scripts/dump-mulberry32.ts after Phase 3 engine lands)

walk_jitter.description: Jitter behavior. expected_pcs values FILLED_BY_ENGINE_IMPLEMENTATION once the mulberry32 reference outputs are in place. Cases below describe the structural assertions; concrete pcs emerge from running the engine and copying back.

cases.name: jitter=0 ignores seed
cases.description: With jitter=0, the seed has no effect. Walk identical to walk_deterministic 'PLR cycle' above.

cases.name: jitter=1 ignores cells
cases.description: With jitter=1, every cell op is replaced by a random pick. Result depends only on seed and pos, not on cells contents.

cases.name: fixed seed reproduces
cases.description: Calling walk(state, pos) twice must yield identical triads.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(state, pos) === walk(state, pos) for arbitrary state, pos

cases.name: any-pos restart consistency
cases.description: walk(state, pos=N) must equal the result of advancing the walker step-by-step from pos=0 to N. Cross-target conformance depends on this — Live can resume playback at any position.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(state, N) === stepwise advance from 0 to N
